evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart with error bars clearly demonstrates OursModel wins on all 5 benchmarks; single-column 3.5in width matches Nature/CVPR spec.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are set; however pdffonts shows font resolved to DejaVuSans (system default not overridden), not a preferred publication font. Sizes 6.5-8pt are reasonable.
evidence.palette: Uses Paul Tol-inspired 4-color CB-safe palette (#999999, #4477AA, #EE6677, #228833) plus hatches for B&W; no red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 3.5x2.6in figure with tight_layout; y-axis truncated above 0 (min ~31); 2-column legend in upper-left; no overlapping labels or gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; specific numerical claim (3.3-13.4 pp gains) included; legend inside plot area; top/right spines removed; professional appearance.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF saved with pdf.fonttype=42 and text is selectable (CID TrueType embedded), but font resolved to DejaVuSans rather than a system font explicitly specified.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (40KB), figure.png (59KB), figure.svg (165KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all 5 benchmarks, describes error bar meaning, identifies winner (OursModel), and provides specific quantitative finding (3.3-13.4 pp over strongest baseline).

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe palette combined with hatching ensures the figure is accessible in both color and greyscale reproduction.
Caption is publication-ready: names benchmarks, quantifies gains, and explains design choices (hatching, error bars).
overall_weaknesses: Font resolves to DejaVuSans (matplotlib default) despite font.family='sans-serif'; a named publication font (e.g., 'Helvetica', 'Arial', or 'Liberation Sans') should be specified explicitly.
pdf.fonttype=42 is set but the CID TrueType embedding means text selectability works, yet the font choice is still the matplotlib default—a minor but avoidable reviewer concern.
